messages.content:
[sustainability_agent] Based on EU_TAXONOMY Art.8 (Taxonomy disclosure obligations): Financial market participants, large public interest entities, and non-financial undertakings subject to NFRD/CSRD shall disclose how and to what extent their activities are associated with environmentally sustainable economic activities. Key performance indicators include: taxonomy-eligible activities as proportion of turnover, capex, and opex.

Sources: EU_TAXONOMY Art.8: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32020R0852 | ESRS ESRS_G1: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32023R2772

[sustainability_agent] Based on CSRD Art.3 (Sustainability reporting standards): Member States shall require undertakings to include in the management report information necessary to understand the undertaking's impacts on sustainability matters and information necessary to understand how sustainability matters affect the undertaking. Sustainability reporting shall follow European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) adopted by the Commission.

Sources: SFDR Art.9: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32019R2088 | CSRD Art.3: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2464

No specific provisions found in Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive for topic 'What is CSRD?'. [sustainability_agent] Based on EU_TAXONOMY Art.8 (Taxonomy disclosure obligations): Financial market participants, large public interest entities, and non-financial undertakings subject to NFRD/CSRD shall disclose how and to what extent their activities are associated with environmentally sustainable economic activities.

Sources: CSRD Art.9: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2464 | CSRD Art.1: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2464
